data.title: Tether CEO Criticizes Reliance on Centralized Infrastructure
data.description: According to Odaily, Tether's CEO, Paolo Ardoino, expressed concerns on the X platform regarding the recent outage of Cloudflare. He emphasized the need for future technologies to move away from dependence on centralized infrastructure.

data.title: Bitcoin Adoption Is Just Getting Started — 200x Growth Possible, Tom Lee Says
data.description:
Fundstrat’s Tom Lee told attendees at Binance Blockchain Week that he believes the worst leg of the recent crypto slump is likely over and that markets may be ready for a gradual recovery. He pointed to weakening selling pressure and growing underlying activity as reasons for cautious optimism. Related Reading: A New Era Begins: CFTC Approves Spot Bitcoin On Regulated US Markets Market Sentiment May Be Near A Turning Point According to Lee, mood on the street turned darker after October, with many investors showing fatigue after steady losses. He said the current selling looks closer to exhaustion than to the start of another major decline
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-adoption-is-just-getting-started-200x-growth-possible-tom-lee-says/

data.title: CoinShares dismisses fresh Tether solvency fears after Arthur Hayes warning
data.description:
CoinShares said Tether has $181 billion in reserves and $174. 45 billion in liabilities, showing a $6. 55 billion surplus and no signs of systemic risk.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/coinshares-dismisses-tether-solvency-fears/

data.title: Strive Challenges MSCI's Decision to Exclude Bitcoin Companies from Index
data.description: According to Odaily, Strive, a Bitcoin treasury company, has expressed concerns over MSCI's decision to remove companies like Strategy and other DAT firms from its index. In a letter addressed to MSCI CEO Henry Fernandez this week, Strive argued that this move deviates from the principle of index neutrality. The company urged the index provider to allow the market to determine how to handle companies with significant Bitcoin holdings on their balance sheets. Strive has disclosed that it currently holds over 7,500 Bitcoins, ranking 14th among publicly listed companies with Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: DTCC Says Global Demand and Momentum Are Driving Push for Extended Trading Hours
data.description:
A new DTCC survey shows a major shift in market behavior: 72% of respondents cite global demand and regulatory momentum as the primary forces behind the growing push for extended U. S. trading hours. The findings highlight how 24/7 expectations, shaped heavily by the crypto markets, are influencing traditional market infrastructure
source: https://www.ethnews.com/dtcc-says-global-demand-and-momentum-are-driving-push-for-extended-trading-hours/

data.title: Ethereum Trading Strategy Faces Challenges Amid Price Fluctuations
data.description: According to PANews, blockchain analyst Yu Jin has reported that Huang Licheng, known as Maji, has seen his profits from a leveraged Ethereum (ETH) trading strategy largely diminish. Initially, Maji invested $500,000 to go long on ETH starting at $2,840. When ETH prices rose to $3,200 two days ago, his investment grew to $3.34 million. However, the strategy also increased the liquidation price to $3,000. Early today, ETH prices fell below $3,000, resulting in two liquidations. Consequently, the position's value dropped from $3.34 million to $730,000, with profits nearly wiped out. The remaining position is now just $42 away from the liquidation price.

data.title: Historic Bitcoin Wallets Transfer $178.29 Million After 13 Years
data.description: According to PANews, two Bitcoin wallets, dormant for over 13 years, have transferred 2,000 Bitcoins valued at $178.29 million to new wallets. These Bitcoins were originally minted by Mike Caldwell as Casascius physical Bitcoins between 2011 and 2013.

data.title: Investor Interest in Buying Crypto Falls as Awareness Hits Record High
data.description:
A new FINRA study shows a surprising shift in investor attitudes toward digital assets: while awareness of cryptocurrencies has never been higher, the share of investors actually considering buying crypto has declined sharply since 2021. The study highlights a widening gap between recognition of the asset class and the willingness to take exposure.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/investor-interest-in-buying-crypto-falls-as-awareness-hits-record-high/
